clusters.neutral_headline: ‘Trump forced Israel into a ceasefire’ with Lebanon
bullet_summary: Lebanon's army soon after reported “acts of aggression” by Israel that it said violated the ceasefire agreement

items.title: Israel's 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon begins
items.standfirst: The leaders of Lebanon and Israel agreed to begin a 10-day ceasefire starting at 5 p.m. Eastern Time.
items.content: The leaders of Lebanon and Israel agreed to begin a 10-day ceasefire starting at 5 p.m. Eastern Time.

items.title: ‘Trump forced Israel into a ceasefire’ with Lebanon
items.standfirst: Abed Abou Shhadeh, a political commentator in Israel, says the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon is ‘problematic'.
items.content: Abed Abou Shhadeh, a political commentator in Israel, says the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon is ‘problematic'.

items.title: Middle East war live: Lebanese army accuses Israel of ceasefire violations
items.standfirst: A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, mediated by the US, went into effect at 5pm EST on Thursday. Lebanon's army soon after reported “acts of aggression” by Israel that it said violated the ceasefire agreement. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, mediated by the US, went into effect at 5pm EST on Thursday. Lebanon's army soon after reported “acts of aggression” by Israel that it said violated the ceasefire agreement. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: 10-day Lebanon-Israel ceasefire begins after weeks of conflict
items.standfirst: A ten-day ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel took effect on Friday as US President Donald Trump pushes for broader regional diplomacy, including a possible first-ever leaders’ meeting. The truce comes amid efforts to end wider conflict involving Iran and follows weeks of intense fighting and heavy casualties.
items.content: A ten-day ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel took effect on Friday as US President Donald Trump pushes for broader regional diplomacy, including a possible first-ever leaders’ meeting. The truce comes amid efforts to end wider conflict involving Iran and follows weeks of intense fighting and heavy casualties.

items.title: 4/16: The Takeout with Major Garrett
items.standfirst: Ceasefire begins between Israel and Lebanon; U.S. blockade of Iranian ports continues for a fourth day.
items.content: Ceasefire begins between Israel and Lebanon; U.S. blockade of Iranian ports continues for a fourth day.

items.title: Iran war live: Ceasefire starts in Lebanon as Trump says Tehran deal close

items.content: Death toll from Israeli attacks on Lebanon reached 2,196 on Thursday as 10-day ceasefire announced.

items.title: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire could impact U.S. deal with Iran
items.standfirst: Israel has been fighting the Iranian regime and its proxy Hezbollah in Lebanon across the border. That local ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon could affect U.S. efforts to come to a broader regional agreement with Iran. Charlie D'Agata reports.
items.content: Israel has been fighting the Iranian regime and its proxy Hezbollah in Lebanon across the border. That local ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon could affect U.S. efforts to come to a broader regional agreement with Iran. Charlie D'Agata reports.

items.title: People in Beirut wary of trusting Israel will uphold Lebanon ceasefire
items.standfirst: Lebanon’s residents say they are wary of trusting that Israel will abide by the ceasefire agreement announced by Trump.
items.content: Lebanon’s residents say they are wary of trusting that Israel will abide by the ceasefire agreement announced by Trump.

items.title: Israel and Lebanon’s 10-day ceasefire goes into effect
items.standfirst: A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon has gone into effect. It began at midnight local time (21:00 GMT).
items.content: A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon has gone into effect. It began at midnight local time (21:00 GMT).

items.title: 4/16: CBS Evening News
items.standfirst: Trump attempts to shift focus off of the war; Israel and Lebanon reach a ceasefire agreement.
items.content: Trump attempts to shift focus off of the war; Israel and Lebanon reach a ceasefire agreement.

items.title: Israel's Netanyahu says he agrees to a 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon
items.standfirst: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday he has agreed to a 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon. In a video statement, Netanyahu said he was taking the step in an attempt “to advance” peace efforts with Lebanon. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.
items.content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday he has agreed to a 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon. In a video statement, Netanyahu said he was taking the step in an attempt “to advance” peace efforts with Lebanon. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.

items.title: Live: Israel’s Netanyahu confirms he agreed to 10-day ceasefire with Lebanon
Israel and Lebanon agree to 10-day ceasefire
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump announced that the leaders of Israel and Lebanon had reached a ceasefire deal as fighting raged between Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah.
items.content: President Donald Trump announced that the leaders of Israel and Lebanon had reached a ceasefire deal as fighting raged between Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah.

items.title: Lebanon president had phone call with Trump, thanked him for 'efforts' towards ceasefire
items.standfirst: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun held a phone call with Donald Trump on Thursday during which he thanked the US leader for his "efforts" to secure a ceasefire with Israel, the presidency in Beirut said. The call comes after Aoun rejected a US request for a "direct call" with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, according to an official Lebanese source, and a day after Trump announced an expected call between the two countries' "leaders". FRANCE 24's Cyril Payen reports from Beirut, Lebanon.
items.content: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun held a phone call with Donald Trump on Thursday during which he thanked the US leader for his "efforts" to secure a ceasefire with Israel, the presidency in Beirut said. The call comes after Aoun rejected a US request for a "direct call" with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, according to an official Lebanese source, and a day after Trump announced an expected call between the two countries' "leaders". FRANCE 24's Cyril Payen reports from Beirut, Lebanon.

clusters.title: Singer d4vd arrested on suspicion of murder in death of Celeste Rivas, LAPD says
clusters.neutral_headline: Singer D4vd arrested in connection with death of missing teen girl
bullet_summary: Singer and songwriter d4vd has been arrested on suspicion of murder for the death of a 14-year-old whose dismembered body was found last year in a Tesla belonging to the singer
The 21-year-old Houston-born singer, whose real name is David Burke, had been under a secret investigation by an LA County grand jury after a 14-year-old girl's decomposed body was found in his car
The remains of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez were found in the singer's car last year

items.title: D4vd arrested on suspicion of killing teen girl whose body was found in his Tesla
items.standfirst: Musician, born David Anthony Burke, arrested in Los Angeles over the death of Celeste Rivas Hernandez, who went missing in 2024R&B singer D4vd has been arrested in connection with the killing of a teenage girl whose severely decomposed body was found in his Tesla, Los Angeles police said on Thursday.The 21-year-old musician, who was born David Anthony Burke, is being held without bail, according to city authorities. Continue reading...

clusters.title: South African opposition leader Malema gets jail sentence
clusters.neutral_headline: South African politician Julius Malema given five-year jail term for gun offence
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World, Guardian World and others

items.title: South African opposition leader Malema gets jail sentence
items.standfirst: In tonight's edition, South Africa’s firebrand, leftist leader Julius Malema is sentenced to five years in prison convicted of firearm offences. Also, on a visit to Cameroon, Pope Leo condemns so-called "tyrants" driving global wars and the exploitation of Africa’s resources. And we’ll have a look at a new online database shaking up the restitution debate.
items.content: In tonight's edition, South Africa’s firebrand, leftist leader Julius Malema is sentenced to five years in prison convicted of firearm offences. Also, on a visit to Cameroon, Pope Leo condemns so-called "tyrants" driving global wars and the exploitation of Africa’s resources. And we’ll have a look at a new online database shaking up the restitution debate.

items.title: South African opposition figure Malema sentenced to five years in prison for firing a gun
items.standfirst: Malema is appealing against the decision, which means he is not going to prison immediately.
items.content: Malema is appealing against the decision, which means he is not going to prison immediately.

items.title: South African politician Julius Malema given five-year jail term for gun offence
items.standfirst: Leader of leftwing Economic Freedom Fighters was convicted last year for firing rifle in the air at 2018 rallyThe South African leftwing politician Julius Malema has been sentenced to five years in prison for firing a rifle in the air at a political rally in 2018.Lawyers for the leader of the Economic Freedom Fighters, South Africa’s fourth largest political party, immediately appealed, and Malema will remain free while the appeal proceedings are under way. Continue reading...
items.content: Leader of leftwing Economic Freedom Fighters was convicted last year for firing rifle in the air at 2018 rallyThe South African leftwing politician Julius Malema has been sentenced to five years in prison for firing a rifle in the air at a political rally in 2018.Lawyers for the leader of the Economic Freedom Fighters, South Africa’s fourth largest political party, immediately appealed, and Malema will remain free while the appeal proceedings are under way. Continue reading...

items.content: A South African opposition leader has been handed a five-year prison sentence for firing a rifle in the air at a rally.

items.title: ICE acting director Todd Lyons will resign at end of May, DHS says
items.standfirst: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement acting director Todd Lyons, a key executor of President Donald Trump's mass deportations agenda, will resign at the end of May, federal officials announced.
items.content: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement acting director Todd Lyons, a key executor of President Donald Trump's mass deportations agenda, will resign at the end of May, federal officials announced.(Image credit: Tom Brenner)

items.title: Acting ICE director Todd Lyons will step down at the end of May, says DHS
items.standfirst: Lyons, who led agency since March 2025, to resign after turbulent year carrying out Trump’s immigration agendaTodd Lyons, the acting director of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), is stepping down after a turbulent year carrying out Donald Trump’s immigration agenda.Lyons, who has been leading the agency since March 2025, will resign at the end of May and move to the private sector, Markwayne ​Mullin, the Department of Homeland Security secretary, said in a statement on Thursday. Continue reading...

items.title: Bank robbers hold 25 hostage - and use sewer to flee with loot
items.standfirst: Armed robbers have targeted a bank in the Italian city of Naples - holding 25 people hostage before escaping through a tunnel.
items.content: Armed robbers have targeted a bank in the Italian city of Naples - holding 25 people hostage before escaping through a tunnel.

items.title: Armed robbers hold 25 people hostage at Naples bank before fleeing through hole in floor
items.standfirst: Thieves believed to have escaped into sewers after holding staff and customers in Crédit Agricole branch for two hoursArmed robbers held 25 people hostage at a bank in Naples for two hours on Thursday, before fleeing through a tunnel.The three thieves entered a branch of Crédit Agricole in the southern Italian city at about 11.30am, taking hostage staff and customers, who were freed by police a couple of hours later. Continue reading...

clusters.title: France arrests suspect over 1982 attack on Jewish restaurant
clusters.neutral_headline: France arrests suspect over 1982 attack on Jewish restaurant

items.title: France arrests suspect over 1982 attack on Jewish restaurant
items.standfirst: Mahmoud Khader Abed Adra, sought for over four decades, was surrendered by Palestinian authorities.
items.content: Mahmoud Khader Abed Adra, sought for over four decades, was surrendered by Palestinian authorities.

items.title: Suspect in 1982 Paris Jewish restaurant attack extradited to France
items.standfirst: A suspect in the 1982 Paris Jewish restaurant attack that killed six people was extradited to France on Thursday by the Palestinian Authority. Hicham Harb, now 72, was detained on arrival in Paris and is due to face formal proceedings over one of the country’s deadliest post-war antisemitic attacks.
items.content: A suspect in the 1982 Paris Jewish restaurant attack that killed six people was extradited to France on Thursday by the Palestinian Authority. Hicham Harb, now 72, was detained on arrival in Paris and is due to face formal proceedings over one of the country’s deadliest post-war antisemitic attacks.

items.title: Democrat Analilia Mejia projected to win N.J. House District 11 seat
items.standfirst: CBS News projects that Democrat Analilia Mejia will win the special election in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District, a seat formerly held by Gov. Mikie Sherrill.
items.content: CBS News projects that Democrat Analilia Mejia will win the special election in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District, a seat formerly held by Gov. Mikie Sherrill.

items.title: Progressive Democrat Analilia Mejia wins New Jersey special election for US House
items.standfirst: Victory means Democrats hold on to the 11th district seat in the House, where Republicans hold a thin majorityDemocrat Analilia Mejia won a New Jersey special election for the US House on Thursday, defeating Republican Joe Hathaway on a message of standing up to Donald Trump.Mejia, a former head of the Working Families Alliance who had support from the senator Bernie Sanders, will fill the seat previously held by the Democratic governor Mikie Sherrill and serve until January. Continue reading...
items.content: Victory means Democrats hold on to the 11th district seat in the House, where Republicans hold a thin majorityDemocrat Analilia Mejia won a New Jersey special election for the US House on Thursday, defeating Republican Joe Hathaway on a message of standing up to Donald Trump.Mejia, a former head of the Working Families Alliance who had support from the senator Bernie Sanders, will fill the seat previously held by the Democratic governor Mikie Sherrill and serve until January. bullet_summary: Reported by BBC Top Stories, Guardian World

items.title: Top Foreign Office official to leave post after Mandelson vetting row
items.standfirst: Sir Olly Robbins has effectively been sacked after his department did not inform the prime minister that Lord Mandelson had failed security vetting.
items.content: Sir Olly Robbins has effectively been sacked after his department did not inform the prime minister that Lord Mandelson had failed security vetting.

items.title: Foreign Office’s top civil servant Olly Robbins forced out over Mandelson vetting row
items.standfirst: Keir Starmer understood to have lost confidence in official over decision to override security vetting failureMandelson failed vetting but Foreign Office overruled decisionSir Olly Robbins, the UK Foreign Office’s top civil servant, has been forced out of his post after the decision to fail Peter Mandelson during his security vetting was overruled by his department.Robbins was the Foreign Office’s most senior official in late January 2025 when the decision was made, paving the way for Mandelson to become the US ambassador. Continue reading...
items.content: Keir Starmer understood to have lost confidence in official over decision to override security vetting failureMandelson failed vetting but Foreign Office overruled decisionSir Olly Robbins, the UK Foreign Office’s top civil servant, has been forced out of his post after the decision to fail Peter Mandelson during his security vetting was overruled by his department.Robbins was the Foreign Office’s most senior official in late January 2025 when the decision was made, paving the way for Mandelson to become the US ambassador. Continue reading...
